name: Transformers.js (HuggingFace in Browser/Node)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); HuggingFace; ONNX Runtime Web backend

auth.notes: Browser/Node local inference; HuggingFace Hub token for private models

performance.notes: ONNX Runtime Web; WebAssembly CPU or WebGPU; first run downloads model; cached thereafter

agent_readiness.notes: HuggingFace Transformers for JavaScript/TypeScript. Run 1000+ models in browser or Node.js via ONNX Runtime (WebAssembly + WebGPU). pipeline() API mirrors Python transformers. Tasks: text-classification, NER, QA, summarization, translation, text-generation, image-classification, object detection, depth estimation, ASR, zero-shot. v3 supports WebGPU for acceleration. Models auto-downloaded from HuggingFace Hub and cached. Privacy-preserving: inference on client.

gotchas: Model download on first use (tens to hundreds of MB)
WebGPU not supported in all browsers (WebAssembly fallback is slower)
Only ONNX-exported models supported (not all HF models available)
Large models (>500MB) impractical for web deployment
best_when: Privacy-sensitive ML inference running entirely in browser
Node.js services requiring ML without Python dependency
Offline-capable web apps with embedded ML models
TypeScript applications needing NLP/vision without cloud API
avoid_when: Large models (>500MB) in browser (download too slow)
High-throughput server inference (Python vLLM/ONNX Runtime faster)
summary: Transformers.js runs 1000+ HuggingFace models in browser or Node.js via ONNX Runtime. Same pipeline() API as Python transformers. Supports text, vision, audio, and multimodal tasks. v3 adds WebGPU acceleration. Models cached locally after first download. Perfect for privacy-preserving ML inference without cloud APIs. TypeScript support. Impractical for large models (>500MB) in browser due to download size.
